name: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO)
description: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) — extracorporeal life support; includes veno-venous (VV), veno-arterial (VA) and arterio-venous (AV) configurations.

en.name: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO)
en.shortDescription: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) — extracorporeal life support; includes veno-venous (VV), veno-arterial (VA) and arterio-venous (AV) configurations.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) is a form of extracorporeal life support in which blood is pumped out of the body, oxygenated (and cleared of carbon dioxide) through a membrane oxygenator, and returned to the patient — used for severe, potentially reversible respiratory and/or cardiac failure. In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating that the patient was supported on ECMO; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care. The main configurations differ by where blood is drained and returned: veno-venous (VV, respiratory support) and veno-arterial (VA, respiratory + cardiac support).

## Included Concepts

The set is anchored on the SNOMED procedure *Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation* with its descendants, so the configuration sub-types resolve automatically.

- *4052536 / 233573008 — Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (SNOMED)*: the general concept; the default when the configuration is not specified.
- *37206603 / 786453001 — Venovenous extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (SNOMED)*: VV-ECMO — drains and returns venous blood; supports oxygenation/ventilation (respiratory failure).
- *37206601 / 786451004 — Venoarterial extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (SNOMED)*: VA-ECMO — returns blood to an artery; supports both gas exchange and the circulation (cardiac or combined failure).
- *37206602 / 786452006 — Arteriovenous extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (SNOMED)*: AV-ECMO — a pumpless configuration (pumpless extracorporeal lung assist) that draws from an artery and returns to a vein, driven by the patient's own arterial pressure rather than a pump; used mainly for carbon dioxide removal and requiring an adequately functioning heart.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records ECMO without specifying the configuration: *4052536 / 233573008 — Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation*. Map to a specific configuration (VV, VA, AV) only when documented.
- **The ECMO procedure, not the equipment**: the ECMO circuit, membrane oxygenator and pump are devices captured elsewhere if needed; cannulation and blood sampling from the circuit are separate procedures and are not part of this set.

fr.name: Oxygénation par membrane extracorporelle (ECMO)
fr.shortDescription: Oxygénation par membrane extracorporelle (ECMO) — assistance extracorporelle ; inclut les configurations veino-veineuse (VV), veino-artérielle (VA) et artério-veineuse (AV).

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Rename to "Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO)"; anchor on ECMO (4052536) with descendants (VV/VA/AV configurations). Added clinical description. Clarified AV-ECMO (pumpless, arterial-pressure-driven, CO2 removal) with an ELSO reference.
